summary: Attempted to run Usage Tracker app. Discovered fundamental issue with Electron module injection - require('electron') returns string path instead of API object. Even simplest electron apps fail with 'Cannot read properties of undefined'. Tried multiple Electron versions (33.0.0, 33.4.11, 40.1.0) - all have same issue. Created patch script for bundling issue, but core electron module problem prevents app from running.
current_status: Blocked by fundamental Electron installation/environment issue. Electron APIs not being injected into module system.
next_steps: Investigate Windows-specific Electron module resolution issue
Check for global npm/electron configuration conflicts
Consider alternative: use Electron Forge or different build tool
blockers: Electron module APIs not injected - require('electron') returns string instead of object
Systemic issue - even simplest electron app fails
